rulings.Case Title: CROWDSTRIKE, INC. VS. DFND SECURITY, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reconsideration Under Cal. Code Of Civ. Proc. Section 1008(A).
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, April 14, 2026, Line 1, RESPONDENT DFND SECURITY, INC.'s Motion For Reconsideration Under Cal. Code Of Civ. Proc. Section 1008(A). Continued April 30, 2026, to be heard by Judge Van Aken, who heard the underlying motions. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/HEK).

rulings.Case Title: SOSHACE LLC ET AL VS. ANKR PBC
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, April 14, 2026, Line 2, PETITIONER SOSHACE LLC, SOSHACE OU's Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award. Hearing required based on petitioners' contesting the previously issued tentative ruling. The previously issued tentative ruling stated: Petitioners' petition to confirm arbitration award is denied without prejudice to being refiled. Petitioners have not shown that electronic service is a permissible method of serving their petition. rulings.Case Title: MATTHEW WAGNER VS. DOE 1 ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Defendant Usa West Province Of The Society Of Jesus Further Responses To Requests For Admission, Set Number One
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, April 14, 2026, Line 3, PLAINTIFF MATTHEW WAGNER's Motion To Compel Defendant Usa West Province Of The Society Of Jesus Further Responses To Requests For Admission, Set Number One. (Complete tentative ruling emailed to the parties). 2 - Plaintiff Matthew Wagner's motion to compel further responses to set one requests for admission from defendant USA West Province of Society of Jesus is granted in part. USA West must provide verified complete code-compliant further responses to requests for admissions 1-56 for the time period up to and including December 31, 1996, but not thereafter. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/HEK).

rulings.Case Title: MATTHEW WAGNER VS. DOE 1 ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Defendant Usa West Province Of The Society Of Jesus Further Responses To Request For Production, Set Number One
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, April 14, 2026, Line 3, PLAINTIFF MATTHEW WAGNER's Motion To Compel Defendant Usa West Province Of The Society Of Jesus Further Responses To Request For Production, Set Number One. (Complete tentative ruling emailed to the parties). 3 - Plaintiff Matthew Wagner's motion to compel further responses to his set one requests for production of documents from defendant USA West Province of Society of Jesus is denied. The way requests 85-88 are framed necessarily implicate attorney-client privilege requiring legal advice from counsel as to what constitutes an entity's knowledge and notice that sexual abuse was committed by its agents and employees. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/HEK).

rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, April 14, 2026, Line 3, PLAINTIFF MATTHEW WAGNER's Motion To Compel Defendant Usa West Province Of The Society Of Jesus Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set Number One. rulings.Case Title: RONNIE BIDDLE III VS. UNIFIT AVIATION, LLC ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, April 14, 2026, Line 6, PLAINTIFF RONNIE BIDDLE III's Joint Motion To Dismiss. Hearing required. At the hearing the court will inquire what compensation, if any, was paid to plaintiff and/or his counsel for the dismissal and whether there is a settlement agreement or other document reflecting the terms on which the dismissal is based and, if so, to provide a copy to the court. Depending on the answers to these questions, the hearing may need to be continued to allow for further papers to be filed regarding the motion. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. rulings.Case Title: JPMORGAN CHASE BANK, N.A. VS. APRIL HAMILTON-WEST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Enter Judgment Under Terms Of Stipulated Settlement
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, April 14, 2026, Line 7, PLAINTIFF JPMORGAN CHASE BANK, N.A.'s Motion To Enter Judgment Under Terms Of Stipulated Settlement. Plaintiff JPMorgan Chase Bank, N.A.'s motion to enter judgment under terms of stipulated settlement is granted. Good cause shown and no opposition filed. JPMorgan is entitled to judgment in its favor and against defendant April Hamilton-West in the amount of $35,171.72 and no costs. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, April 14, 2026, Line 8, PLAINTIFF FLEXGROUND, LLC's Amended APPLICATION AND HEARING FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER. Plaintiff Flexground, LLC's application for right to attach order is denied without prejudice to being refiled with proper notice.
